scope: ACL27 supplement Figure S25 material claim-boundary gate footer
user_report: The bottom concept diagram in supplement Figure S25 was not fully displayed.
root_cause: The standalone atlas generator placed the complete AI claim-boundary gate slot with cover-style scaling in the footer. The 1672x941 source schematic was therefore cropped inside a wider fixed footer slot before LaTeX saw the PNG; this was not a LaTeX page crop.
changes: Changed _draw_material_claim_boundary_gate_footer to place MATERIAL_CLAIM_BOUNDARY_GATE_AI_SLOT with contain/letterbox scaling rather than cover scaling.
Added a layout regression assertion rejecting cover=True for the material claim-boundary gate footer block.
Regenerated fig_supplement_material_claim_boundary_atlas.png and rebuilt the ACL27 supplement PDF.

local_visual_review.reviewer: Codex local visual review using render-visual-reviewer paper figure protocol; no subagent spawned because this environment only permits spawn_agent when explicitly requested.
local_visual_review.standalone_verdict: PASS. The bottom claim-boundary gate schematic shows all three input rows, the center gate, and all three output boxes inside the figure frame.
local_visual_review.pdf_page_27_verdict: PASS. Figure S25 on supplement page 27 keeps the bottom concept diagram fully visible and the caption remains below the figure without collision.
local_visual_review.residual_caveat: The AI schematic is now slightly smaller in its footer frame because the full source aspect ratio is preserved. This is preferable to clipping an exposition-only slot.

follow_up_risks: This fix closes Figure S25 only. Other supplement figures with AI slots or wide media strips should keep using contain/letterbox placement unless a deliberate crop is explicitly justified.
The broader ACL/supplement visual review should continue treating obvious clipping, black padded tiles, and unreadable scaled panels as release blockers.
